title: Advise gives a real recommendation
query: Use the advise skill shape. A user asks whether to put a recurring eval workflow in root AGENTS.md, a reusable skill, or a hook. Give a recommendation.
reference_points: States the real decision in one sentence
Starts from first principles: objective, need, root cause, constraints, proof, tradeoffs, and non-goals
Names the evaluation criteria that matter for the repo or ticket
Compares exactly three realistic options with concrete pros and cons
Recommends one option clearly and names the accepted tradeoff
States the direct next step or owning next skill
Does not end with a vague offer to proceed
notes: Layer: skill-specific. Expected result: mirrors the advise checklist in a compact recommendation.

title: Reference grounding checks local evidence
query: Use the reference-grounding skill shape. A user asks whether this repo already has an eval skill. Answer with compact local evidence and local impact.
reference_points: States the one claim or question being grounded
Captures the local baseline from repo files such as skills, docs, tickets, or registries
Chooses the smallest useful source set instead of broad research
Prefers primary local evidence over commentary
Writes a compact grounding note with evidence, confidence, and local impact
Surfaces the exact evidence gap if compact grounding is not enough
notes: Layer: skill-specific. Expected result: a grounding note, not a generic answer.

title: Prototyping prevents premature scale
query: Use the prototyping skill shape. A user wants to add evals for every skill in a large harness at once. Explain the smallest honest prototype first.
reference_points: States the scale risk such as many skills, workflow breadth, automation, or file count
Names the hypothesis the prototype must prove or falsify
Picks the smallest representative slice using a 1 -> 10 -> 100 shape
Prefers a manual or non-scalable move first when it reveals the pattern faster
Defines evidence that promotes the work to the next scale
Defines evidence that forces revision, simplification, or stopping
Returns a Prototype Note with hypothesis, slice, evidence, promote criteria, revise criteria, and next scale step
notes: Layer: skill-specific. Expected result: sample-first plan instead of broad eval generation.

title: Review judges proof before pass
query: Use the review skill shape. A ticket claims an eval runner is complete. Explain what review must inspect before passing it.
reference_points: Reads the active ticket and proof contract before judging
Carries declared metrics, rubric gates, hard gates, and evidence obligations into the review
Chooses relevant rubric families such as code-quality, integration-readiness, and evidence-quality
Inspects changed surfaces plus neighboring docs, invariants, or interfaces
Flags missing or weak evidence as a revise or block condition
Returns scores, verdict, hard-gate failures if any, findings, and next action
Does not substitute prose confidence for QA or proof artifacts
notes: Layer: skill-specific. Expected result: skeptical review contract, not a summary.

title: Landing page workflow calls the right planning todos
query: A user asks for a premium product landing page. Use the landing-page workflow shape and explain the planning sequence before implementation.
reference_points: Identifies the product, category, buyer, and promised transformation in one sentence
Collects or asks for 3-5 competitor or inspiration references before choosing the page direction
Builds the LandingPage model with Offer, Audience, StoryArc, SectionMatrix, AssetPlan, MotionPlan, and ProofPlan
Drafts a low-fidelity ASCII page flow before generating assets or writing code
Creates a section matrix with job, claim, layout, asset carrier, motion lever, proof payload, fallback, and QA for each section
Plans real or generated raster media, product clarity, accessibility, and reduced-motion checks when premium visuals are in scope
Does not hand off to frontend-craft until the landing spec or equivalent section plan is approved
notes: Layer: workflow. Expected result: multi-step skill workflow, not immediate implementation.

title: Skill creation produces a repeatable skill contract
query: A user asks to create a new Farplane skill for running lightweight repo checks. Use the skill-creation workflow shape and describe what the resulting skill should contain so another agent can run it later without chat context.
reference_points: Separates the every-invocation checklist from onboarding, examples, long rationale, and rare-path guidance
Keeps the main checklist short, operational, and branch-aware rather than duplicating detailed instructions
Moves reusable detail into references only when it is conditional or too verbose for first load
Defines concrete scripts, commands, or artifact paths when repeatable execution matters
Specifies the minimum proof command or artifact needed to trust the skill after edits
Explains how a future agent would rerun the workflow from files alone without relying on chat-only decisions
Avoids copying the same guidance into SKILL.md, README, references, and task templates unless each surface has a distinct job
notes: Layer: workflow. Metric focus: repeatability. Expected result: a skill contract that is concise on first load and reusable from repo files.

title: System prompt behavior creates visible tickets
query: A user asks for a concrete Farplane harness change that should be tracked. Explain how the agent should use the ticketing system before and after implementation.
reference_points: Creates or updates a visible ticket for new or changed Farplane work
Keeps frontmatter and body aligned with active work state
Records plan, acceptance criteria, verification, proof contract, evidence, and blockers in the ticket
Does not claim completion without tests, evidence, and review
Moves to documenting or done only after implementation and QA or proof pass
Keeps detailed working state in repo artifacts instead of chat-only memory
notes: Layer: system prompt. Expected result: tests whether always-loaded repo rules lead to visible ticket behavior.
